paragraphs.en: It is seven o'clock. Time to get up! You have school this morning.
paragraphs.fr: Il est sept heures. C'est l'heure de se lever ! Tu as ecole ce matin.
paragraphs.speaker: Mum

paragraphs.en: OK! I get up at seven. I eat breakfast at half past seven. Then I go to school.
paragraphs.fr: D'accord ! Je me leve a sept heures. Je prends le petit-dejeuner a sept heures et demie. Puis je vais a l'ecole.
paragraphs.speaker: Ben

paragraphs.en: What time do you eat lunch?
paragraphs.fr: A quelle heure manges-tu le dejeuner ?
paragraphs.speaker: Mum

paragraphs.en: I eat lunch at twelve o'clock. In the evening, I do my homework and I go to bed early.
paragraphs.fr: Je mange le dejeuner a midi. Le soir, je fais mes devoirs et je me couche tot.
paragraphs.speaker: Ben

vocabulary.definition_en: la premiere partie de la journee, avant midi
vocabulary.example: I go to school in the morning.

vocabulary.definition_en: un endroit ou les enfants apprennent
le repas que l'on mange au milieu de la journee
la partie de la journee entre l'apres-midi et la nuit
vocabulary.example: In the evening, I watch TV.
The clock says it is three o'clock.
I go to bed early on school days.

grammar_points.title: Present simple: I get up, I go, I eat
grammar_points.explanation: Le **present simple** s'utilise pour parler de ce que l'on fait tous les jours (habitudes et routines). Avec **I, you, we, they**, le verbe ne change pas. En français, cela correspond à 'je me lève', 'je vais', 'je mange'.
examples: I get up at seven. = Je me leve a sept heures.
I go to school. = Je vais a l'ecole.
I eat lunch at noon. = Je mange le dejeuner a midi.
I do my homework. = Je fais mes devoirs.

expressions.example: Excuse me, what time is it? It's three o'clock.
expressions.note: La façon standard de demander l'heure. Réponse : 'It's ... o'clock'.

expressions.example: It's seven o'clock. Time for school!
expressions.note: S'utilise pour dire l'heure exacte. 'O'clock' ne s'emploie que pour les heures pleines (7:00, pas 7:30).

written.question: How do you say 'Quelle heure est-il ?' in English?
written.explanation: The correct form is 'What time is it?' using 'what' (not 'how' or 'where').

written.question: Which sentence describes a daily routine?
options: I eat lunch at twelve o'clock.
written.explanation: 'I eat lunch at twelve o'clock' uses the present simple, which describes a regular habit.

written.sentence: I ________ up at seven o'clock every morning.
written.hint: The verb for 'se lever' in English: to get up.

written.sentence: In the ________, I do my homework.

written.french: Je vais a l'ecole le matin. Je mange le dejeuner a midi.
written.suggested_answer: I go to school in the morning. I eat lunch at twelve o'clock.

oral.instruction: Read these sentences aloud slowly and clearly.
oral.text: I get up at seven o'clock. I go to school in the morning. I eat lunch at noon.
oral.focus: Practice the rhythm of each sentence. Stress the time words: 'seven', 'morning', 'noon'.

oral.question: What time do you get up? What do you do in the morning?
oral.suggested_answer: I get up at seven o'clock. In the morning, I eat breakfast and I go to school.
